intro: Psychotria calorhamnus, also known as Hawaiian island torchwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reduce the severity of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria calorhamnus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian herbalism, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
